claim: ElasticSearch and OpenSearch connectors (~80% identical, normalized diff 258/1295 lines) should merge into one Koan.Data.SearchEngine-based connector with a small dialect seam (auth header, distance metric names, knn query shape), saving ~1.3k LOC and one project.
corrections: 'One package/one project saved' is REFUTED: Reference=Intent plus per-backend [KoanService] orchestration metadata (different ContainerImage/DefaultTag/Env: docker.elastic.co elasticsearch 8.13.4 + xpack.security.enabled=false vs opensearchproject/opensearch 2.13.0 + DISABLE_SECURITY_PLUGIN=true) require two thin packages to remain. The shared base goes into the already-existing, already-referenced Koan.Data.SearchEngine — project count is unchanged (and no new project is needed).

'Auth header' is NOT part of the dialect seam: ConfigureHttpClient is byte-identical in both connectors (ApiKey scheme + Basic fallback; ES:619-641 = OS:506-528). The real seam is exactly three things: (1) search-request body builder (ES 8.x top-level knn w/ field/query_vector/num_candidates/knn.filter vs OS 2.x query.knn.<field> w/ vector/k + bool-filter wrapper), (2) index settings+mapping body (dense_vector+similarity vs index.knn=true + knn_vector + method{hnsw,lucene,space_type}), (3) similarity-metric token mapping (MapSpaceType). Plus per-backend factory/discovery/registrar metadata, which stays in the thin packages anyway. Caveat: the identical ApiKey branch on OS is likely dead/wrong (OS does not use the Elasticsearch 'ApiKey' Authorization scheme) — fix during the merge, don't template it.

LOC saving is ~1.0-1.1k, not ~1.3k: measured normalized diff is 300 changed of 1427 ES-side lines (claim's 258/1295 is the right ballpark); post-merge totals ≈ base 1100-1200 + 2 shims of 200-280 vs current 2674. And ~144 of the 235 changed repo-file lines are not dialect but a one-sided FEATURE GAP (ES-only ExportAll scroll export, GetCount, IndexStats) — the genuine dialect surface is only ~70-90 lines per side, confined to two body-builder methods and one mapper, with identical control flow everywhere. This makes the shared-base design BETTER than the claim suggests, not worse — attack vector 1 inverts in the claim's favor.

The merge must explicitly supersede DATA-0097 §6, which deliberately kept the repositories separate with the rationale 'their REST/transport wiring differs' (DATA-0097-vector-pathway-parity.md:138-142). That rationale is empirically false: the REST/transport wiring (HttpClient config, auth, NDJSON bulk envelope, _doc PUT/DELETE, _delete_by_query, probe-then-create index flow) is the byte-identical part; only the two JSON body builders differ. The ADR's own S3 finding ('repos ~95% identical') already contradicted its §6 conclusion.

The claim under-sells the strongest evidence — twin maintenance has ALREADY produced bugs: (1) OpenSearchVectorAdapterFactory.CanHandle also claims provider 'elastic' at the same ProviderPriority(20) as the ES factory → ambiguous provider resolution when both are referenced; (2) OS lacks ExportAll/GetCount/IndexStats and falls back to a throwing default-interface-method whose message tells users to switch to ElasticSearch, even though OpenSearch supports the identical scroll API — vector-migration capability silently lost; (3) options drift (ES dead DefaultTopK/MaxTopK vs OS DefaultPageSize=50 with different IAdapterOptions semantics); (4) ES registrar emits sourceKey boot-report provenance, OS was never back-ported; (5) OS comments literally say '(same fix as Elasticsearch)' — manual fix-mirroring is the process. The shared base should implement ExportAll/IndexStats once for both engines, closing the gap.

Alternative (c) (status quo + shared test battery) is already the status quo — VectorAdapterSurface TestKit matrix specs (Surface/Partition/Semantic/FilterConvergence) run against both connectors — and it demonstrably did not prevent the drift above; per-adapter suites structurally cannot catch the dual-'elastic' cross-claim or an untested NotSupported default method. Alternative (b) (one config-selected package) breaks Reference=Intent and the [KoanService] one-attribute-one-container model. Only (a) fits framework canon.
evidence: src/Connectors/Data/ElasticSearch/ElasticSearchVectorRepository.cs:423-453 — ES 8.x BuildSearchRequest: top-level knn {field, query_vector, k, num_candidates} + knn.filter (DATA-0097 F6 fix)

src/Connectors/Data/OpenSearch/OpenSearchVectorRepository.cs:301-344 — OS 2.x BuildSearchRequest: query.knn.<field> {vector, k} + filter.bool.filter wrapper; comment documents ES shape is rejected by OS

src/Connectors/Data/ElasticSearch/ElasticSearchVectorRepository.cs:562-587 vs src/Connectors/Data/OpenSearch/OpenSearchVectorRepository.cs:425-458 — index provisioning bodies: dense_vector/dims/similarity vs index.knn=true + knn_vector/dimension/method{hnsw,lucene,space_type}; surrounding EnsureIndex control flow identical

src/Connectors/Data/ElasticSearch/ElasticSearchVectorRepository.cs:619-641 = src/Connectors/Data/OpenSearch/OpenSearchVectorRepository.cs:506-528 — ConfigureHttpClient byte-identical (ApiKey + Basic): 'auth header' is not a dialect difference
src/Connectors/Data/ElasticSearch/ElasticSearchVectorRepository.cs:283-392,499-523,410-418 — ES-only ExportAll (scroll), GetCount, IndexStats: ~144 lines of the diff are a feature gap, not dialect

src/Koan.Data.Vector.Abstractions/IVectorSearchRepository.cs:66-74 — ExportAll default interface method throws NotSupported and recommends 'ElasticSearch, Weaviate, Qdrant' — what OS users currently hit

src/Connectors/Data/OpenSearch/OpenSearchVectorAdapterFactory.cs:36-38 + src/Connectors/Data/ElasticSearch/ElasticSearchVectorAdapterFactory.cs:12,36-38 — BOTH factories claim provider 'elastic' at ProviderPriority(20): live copy-paste cross-claim

src/Connectors/Data/ElasticSearch/ElasticSearchOptions.cs:34-45 vs src/Connectors/Data/OpenSearch/OpenSearchOptions.cs:34-35 — options drift (ES DefaultTopK/MaxTopK referenced nowhere else = dead config; OS plain DefaultPageSize=50)
src/Connectors/Data/ElasticSearch/Initialization/KoanAutoRegistrar.cs:127-178 — six sourceKey: provenance args present only on the ES side (OS registrar lacks them)
src/Connectors/Data/OpenSearch/OpenSearchVectorRepository.cs:35-38 — comment '(same fix as Elasticsearch)': manual fix-mirroring demonstrated in-code

docs/decisions/DATA-0097-vector-pathway-parity.md:138-142,219-220,240-241 — §6 deliberately kept repositories separate, rationale 'their REST/transport wiring differs' (empirically false); :78 S3 'repos ~95% identical'

src/Koan.Data.SearchEngine/SearchEngineFilterTranslator.cs:9-36 — shared Lucene-DSL translator already extracted with an 'engine' label param: the dialect-parameterized pattern is precedented in this exact assembly

src/Connectors/Data/ElasticSearch/Koan.Data.Connector.ElasticSearch.csproj:19-23 = src/Connectors/Data/OpenSearch/Koan.Data.Connector.OpenSearch.csproj:19-23 — identical client stacks (IHttpClientFactory raw HttpClient, Newtonsoft 13.0.4, M.E.Http 10.0.8) and both already reference Koan.Data.SearchEngine

src/Connectors/Data/ElasticSearch/ElasticSearchVectorAdapterFactory.cs / OpenSearchVectorAdapterFactory.cs [KoanService] attrs — per-backend container image/tag/env/shortCode: why one merged package cannot carry the orchestration metadata

tests/Suites/Data/VectorAdapterSurface/Koan.Data.VectorAdapterSurface.OpenSearch.Tests/OpenSearchMatrixSpecs.cs:1-23 — shared TestKit matrix already runs against both backends (alternative (c) already exists and did not prevent drift)

Measured: normalized diff 300 changed / 1427 ES-side lines (~79% identical; per-file: VectorRepository 235/704, Registrar 19/182, Discovery 16/129, Options 14/49, Factory 14/62, Configurator 2/189; HealthContributor/Constants/Telemetry/csproj 0 changed)
refinedRecommendation: Extract a shared SearchEngine vector connector core into the existing Koan.Data.SearchEngine assembly (which both connectors already reference), keeping BOTH thin per-backend packages — do not collapse to one package. Concretely: (1) move the entire identical skeleton into a base SearchEngineVectorRepository<TEntity,TKey> (or a concrete repo + ISearchEngineDialect strategy object, preferred for composition): upsert/bulk-NDJSON/delete/_delete_by_query/probe-then-create EnsureIndex flow/hit parsing/Flush/IndexName/HttpClient+auth config/Id normalization — all byte-identical today; (2) the dialect seam is exactly three members: BuildSearchRequestBody (ES 8.x top-level knn vs OS 2.x query.knn.<field>), BuildIndexBody (dense_vector/similarity vs index.knn+knn_vector/method), and MapSimilarityToken (MapSpaceType) — NOT auth, which is identical (and audit the OS ApiKey branch as likely dead); (3) implement ExportAll (scroll), GetCount, and IndexStats once in the base — OpenSearch supports the same scroll API, so this FIXES the current OS capability gap instead of preserving it, and aligns with DATA-0097 §7's capability-flag direction; (4) keep per-backend packages owning only: [KoanService] orchestration metadata (container image/tag/env), provider shortCode + CanHandle, discovery adapter (env-var names, aliases), options binding path, and registrar describe entries — preserving Reference=Intent; (5) while merging, fix the drift bugs the twins already accumulated: remove 'elastic' from OpenSearchVectorAdapterFactory.CanHandle, de-duplicate the shared 'search' discovery alias, unify the IAdapterOptions page-size semantics (delete the dead DefaultTopK/MaxTopK or actually use them), and back-port sourceKey boot-report provenance to OS; (6) record this as a DDR that explicitly supersedes DATA-0097 §6, on the grounds that its 'REST/transport wiring differs' rationale is contradicted by the code (the wiring is the identical part). Expected outcome: ~1.0-1.1k LOC removed, zero project-count change, two real bugs fixed, OS gains export/stats parity, and the existing VectorAdapterSurface TestKit matrix (which already runs both backends) becomes the regression net for the refactor.
